title: fix: add missing ltx-2 routing in video handler
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Plugging LTX-2 back in
gist.blurb: We found a loose wire in our video routing! The LTX-2 video model is fully plugged back in and ready to generate your moving pixels.
gist.summary: Fixed a routing issue in the video generation handler that prevented the LTX-2 model from processing requests.
gist.impact: Users can once again generate videos using the ltx-2 model without encountering unsupported generation errors.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a technician, fixing a loose cable or plugging a glowing wire into a retro video router or TV monitor. The monitor robot companion is present and happy.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, plugging a glowing lime green (#ecf874) cable into a chunky retro video router. A cute monitor robot nearby displays a happy face on its screen. Soft lavender and peach pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
